creator: denverh@comcast.net
text:
Thanks.  I'm pretty sure that this also happened when starting zim for the very first time on a fresh install when there is no data at all.  And I don't seem to have a ~/.zim directory.  There are two index.db files associated with zim:
~/.cache/zim/notebook-usr_local_share_zim_manual/index.db
~/.cache/zim/notebook-home_users_dhull_Notebooks_Notes/index.db

I noticed that when starting it with the culprit line commented out it would always briefly display a notice that it was updating something, possibly the index.  But it would continue to fail the same way when that line was not commented out.  However, I just checked and now that I've added some notes, it runs fine with that line active.  I don't know what happened to cause this, but it seems to be working OK now.

Yes, I know about py311.  After reinstalling everything, mostly from quarterly pkgs, I seem to have a mixture of 3.9 and 3.11.  

I just noticed that I used the word "files" in the description instead of "fails".

Thanks for the help,

Denver
